lines: Two Choir cultists wait where the narthex floor narrows. One holds a guttering candle tray. The other has relic glass wrapped into his fist.
"Ash on your coat," the throat-singer says. "Office steel under it. Before you come farther, name the mouth you serve."
"Then you came with a closed hand," the candle-bearer says.
The throat-singer raises the shard. "The Censure only knows how to seal a door. We open them."

effects.log: The entrance Choir spread apart and come at you.

lines: "The Host that makes confession honest," the throat-singer says.
"The wound that does not let a body lie," says the candle-bearer. "And you?"

choices.label: I serve the bell and the fire

effects.log: The cultists hiss at the old words and reach for their knives.

choices.label: No god in this room gets my knee

effects.log: The candle-bearer lowers the tray. Hot wax spills across his sleeve as he draws his knife.
